title: pyrus aria var. graeca
intro: Pyrus aria var. graeca, also known as the Mountain Ash or Rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the mountainous regions of Greece. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antimicrobial activities. The Mountain Ash has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions, thanks to its high content of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including immune system support and antioxidant properties.
